name: Chatham Lodging Trust
business: Chatham Lodging Trust invests primarily in upscale extended-stay and premium-branded select-service hotels. As of December 31, 2025, the Company owned 33 hotels with 5,021 rooms in 15 states and the District of Columbia. The hotels are managed by third-party management companies, with all currently managed by Island Hospitality Management, LLC, which is owned by the Company's Chairman and CEO.

exposures.sentence: We and our hotel managers rely on information technology in our operations, and any material failure, inadequacy, interruption or security failure of that technology could harm our business.
We may face challenges managing rapidly advancing artificial intelligence in our business which could adversely affect our competitive position.
The increasing use by consumers of Internet travel intermediaries and alternative lodging market places may adversely affect our profitability.

We face risks associated with natural disasters and the direct and indirect physical effects of climate change, which may include more frequent and more severe storms, hurricanes, flooding, droughts and wildfires, any of which could have a material adverse effect on our hotel properties, operations, cash flows and financing options.

catalysts.sentence: We expect to invest approximately $26.5 million on renovations, discretionary and emergency expenditures on our existing hotels in 2026.
The new Credit Facility has an initial maturity date of September 25, 2029.
